pattern: You may repeatedly pick any substring of a digit string and sort it in place; decide whether that lets you turn one digit string into another.
hints: Sorting a substring can only ever remove inversions, never create one, so think of the whole process as a sequence of adjacent swaps that each fix a pair where a larger digit sits before a smaller one.

For each digit value, remember the positions where it appears in the original string, in left-to-right order; matching the target character by character consumes the earliest remaining position for that digit.

Before consuming a digit's position, make sure no strictly smaller digit still has an unused occurrence sitting further left, since that digit would have to be dragged rightward past a smaller one, which no sequence of sorts can do.

edge_inputs.description: s already equals t, no transformation needed
sorting the whole string once reaches strictly ascending order
a long run of one repeated digit
the target reintroduces an inversion that no sequence of sorts could ever create
a smaller digit sits ahead of the position a larger digit needs to be pulled from
digits already close to their target order, needing only local rearrangement

stress.description: random digit strings paired with a shuffled permutation of the same multiset
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, min(size, 60))
    s = ''.join(str(rng.randint(0, 9)) for _ in range(n))
    t_chars = list(s)
    rng.shuffle(t_chars)
    t = ''.join(t_chars)
    return [s, t]
stress.note: kept well under the length cap so the O(n^2) oracle finishes quickly
